what_it_does: Renders each HTML version listed in pieces/manifest.json into a 1600x900 PNG under review/ by making a temporary CSS-adjusted copy, capturing it with headless Chrome at 1600x1000, and cropping the result to 1600x900 with PIL.
observations: Uses bash with set -euo pipefail and cleans a temporary directory on exit.
Derives ROOT from the script location and reads pieces/manifest.json relative to that root.
Creates the review directory before rendering outputs.
A Python snippet emits tab-separated source and output paths from manifest mockups and versions.
Injects a body style override into a temporary HTML file instead of modifying the source HTML.
Runs Chrome headless with --no-sandbox, hidden scrollbars, device scale factor 1, and a 1600x1000 window.
Uses PIL to convert the screenshot to RGB and crop the top-left 1600x900 region.
Prints a rendered line with the output path and actual image dimensions for each file.
standouts: Render-time CSS injection keeps stored HTML files unchanged.
The script deliberately over-renders height to 1000px and crops to 900px to avoid a documented grey-band issue.
Output naming is manifest-driven as review/<mockup-id>.<version-name>.png.
conventions: Uses environment override CHROME with google-chrome as the default.
Combines bash orchestration with small inline Python snippets for JSON parsing, HTML injection, and image cropping.
Fails fast on missing source HTML files and command errors.
Stores generated PNGs in a review directory relative to the project root.
concerns: Depends on python3, PIL, and Chrome being available without preflight checks.
The HTML injection only looks for a literal lowercase </body> closing tag.
Manifest paths containing tab characters would break the tab-separated while-read protocol.
Chrome stdout and stderr are suppressed, which limits diagnostic detail on render failures.
notes: A manifest-driven screenshot renderer with a temporary body override and crop step to produce consistent 1600x900 review images.
questions: What manifest schema guarantees are expected for mockups, versions, and file fields?
Should render failures expose Chrome diagnostics for easier debugging?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it renders every manifest version to a clean 1600x900 PNG, with documented render-time handling for the grey-band issue.
intention_signals: It seems intended to produce reproducible review screenshots while avoiding edits to versioned HTML and compensating for headless Chrome viewport behavior.
novelty: The explicit grey-band workaround combines temporary CSS neutralization, extra viewport height, and top-left cropping.
contribution: Provides a reusable file-local rendering procedure that turns manifest-listed HTML mockups into normalized PNG review artifacts while preserving the original HTML files.
summary_for_embedding: Bash script that reads counterpart-design pieces/manifest.json, renders each listed HTML mockup version with headless Chrome, applies a temporary body override, and saves cropped 1600x900 PNGs to review/.
intention_for_embedding: It was for producing consistent visual review images from HTML mockup versions while avoiding a documented headless Chrome grey-band rendering issue.
